description: In 2000, the Norwegian government-owned Telenor and the Swedish government-owned Telia announced a merger to create a dominant Nordic telecommunications giant. The deal collapsed within months due to cultural clashes, conflicting management structures, and government interference.

key_events.description: Merger announcement hailed as a landmark Nordic economic collaboration.
Public reports emerge regarding internal disagreement on the CEO selection process.
Norwegian and Swedish parliament members voice concerns over loss of national control.
The merger is officially abandoned, cementing failure of the cross-border strategy.

calibration_notes: Useful for modeling how high initial optimism can rapidly erode due to organizational friction and political nationalism.
